p.strong: 16-byte unaligned read crosses page boundary → potential SIGSEGV

ul.li: Safety: Atomic within single page entry.

div.p: Let $P$ be page size ($4096$) and $W$ be read width ($16$). Page boundaries occur at addresses $A$ where $A \equiv 0 \pmod P$.

If address $S$ is aligned to $W$ ($S \equiv 0 \pmod W$), then since $P$ is a multiple of $W$ ($4096 / 16 = 256$), the range $[S, S+W-1]$ cannot contain any address $A \equiv 0 \pmod P$ unless $S$ itself is $A$.
When performing SIMD operations near buffer edges:

ol.li: Use bitwise shifts or _mm_alignr_epi8 to extract the target unaligned bytes.
Never use _mm_loadu_si128 within 15 bytes of a page boundary.
